title: Slow Cooked French Beef Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew made with tender beef, vegetables, and a rich mustardy broth, served in a lacquerware bowl.
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium high heat and brown the beef on all sides.
Remove the browned beef from the skillet and set aside.
Add more oil to the skillet if necessary and saute the onions until they are softened.
Add the carrots and potatoes to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes.
In a small bowl mix together the flour, ferment jang, and mustard.
Add the flour mixture to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the browned beef, beef broth, and salt and pepper to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Transfer the stew to a lacquerware bowl and cook on low in a slow cooker for 8 hours.
